overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research and opportunities in computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. LinkedIn has the highest follower count, but engagement is spread across platforms.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research projects, findings, and methodologies in computational social science.
Content focusing on the detection, analysis, and mitigation of disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media.
Announcements of job openings, research grants, and opportunities for students and researchers.

recommendations: Diversify content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to increase engagement.
Use platform-specific hashtags to reach a wider audience.
Engage with comments and questions to build a stronger community.
Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is used to share research publications and conference presentations, with a focus on topics like coordinated inauthentic behavior and computational methods.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used to share updates on research projects and participate in discussions related to disinformation.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is used for professional announcements, such as research grants and job opportunities. It has the highest follower count and attracts the most likes and comments.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
